subject: Sociology
title: Max Weber and Social Action
description: Learn Max Weber and Social Action in one minute.

hook.text: Why did capitalism explode in Protestant countries first? Weber found the answer in religious belief, not economics.

buildup.text: Weber focused on verstehen — understanding the meaning people attach to their actions, not just patterns.

discovery.text: In The Protestant Ethic, he argued that Calvinist beliefs about salvation fueled disciplined work and saving.

twist.text: Unlike Marx, Weber saw ideas shaping economics, not just the reverse. Culture and economy influence each other.

climax.text: Weber warned that rationalization would trap humanity in an iron cage of bureaucracy and efficiency.

punchline.text: Weber saw that ideas can move economies, not just the reverse.

quiz.question: What did Weber argue fueled the rise of capitalism?
options: Protestant religious beliefs about salvation and work
Purely economic forces and trade routes
